choice: Data-centric immersive dark UI (near-black + deep maroon) responsive mobile web
rationale: Three independent constraints eliminate the other two options. (1) Native mobile app is explicitly listed as OUT of v1 in the v1 screen decision (99d5cbfb), and the stack is Next.js/Tailwind 4 web (01d42543), so 'Native mobile app' is ruled out. (2) 'Light minimal SaaS look' is inconsistent with the explicit non-SaaS, single-household-per-deployment model (1306ca6a) — the product is a personal financial planning tool, not a multi-tenant SaaS product, so the SaaS visual metaphor is a mismatch. (3) The core purpose (Monte Carlo probability surfaces, three-layer tracking chart, actual-vs-plan comparison, net-worth-by-bucket dashboards — 913da4c1, 479caa25, 99d5cbfb) is deeply data-centric and visualization-heavy, which aligns with a dense, immersive dark UI rather than a sparse marketing-oriented light look. With native mobile ruled out by binding decision and the light SaaS look ruled out by the non-SaaS product identity, the data-centric dark responsive web option is the only internally consistent choice. The specific palette (near-black + deep maroon) is not directly bound by the priors but follows by elimination.
